title: Isolating DNA from Wolbachia and Drosophila with the Puregene Tissue Kit
author: Alphaxand Njogu

steps_raw.instruction: Transfer Centrifuge tubes, 1.5 mL, safe-lock, PCR clean Eppendorf Catalog #22363212 containing sample to a bead-mill homogenizer. Homogenize at 1500 rpm, Room temperature, for 00:02:00 .
Add 301.5 µL lysis mixture to each sample.
Transfer samples to an ice block. Incubate for 00:01:00 to quickly cool the samples.

Add 100 µL protein precipitation solution to each sample. Immediately vortex each sample for 00:00:20 . It is important that the samples are immediately mixed after the protein precipitation solution is added. Failing to do so can impact quality of the precipitation.
Centrifuge for 00:03:00 at 16000 rpm, Room temperature, to pellet the protein. If the protein pellet is not tight, incubate On ice for 00:05:00 and repeat the centrifugation.

Create lysis mixture containing the following, per sample in a Centrifuge tubes, 1.5 mL, safe-lock, PCR clean Eppendorf Catalog #22363212 : 300 µL cell lysis solution 1.5 µL Proteinase K Prepare a bit extra to account for pipetting errors.

Mix by inverting the tubes 25 times. To process large batches, arrange tubes in a tube rack. Place a second rack on top, sandwiching the tubes. Invert the entire stack to simultaneously invert all tubes.

Add 300 µL Isopropanol, molecular biology grade Fisher Scientific Catalog #BP-2618-1 to a clean Centrifuge tubes, 1.5 mL, safe-lock, PCR clean Eppendorf Catalog #22363212 . Carefully transfer the supernatant from the prior step to the isopropanol by pipetting.
Add 300 µL of 70 % (v/v) ethanol to each sample.
Add between 15 µL and 100 µL DNA hydration solution to each sample. The choice of elution volume depends on the starting material. For low biomass samples, a lower volume is preferred.
Incubate at 55 °C for between 03:00:00 and 16:00:00 in a dry-bath incubator. (Optional) If using a Thermomixer, set to shake at 300 rpm, °C, .

(Optional) Dilute 20 µg/µL Glycogen, molecular biology grade Invitrogen Catalog #10814-010 to 0.5 µg/µL . Add 1 µL to each sample. Glycogen acts as a carrier molecule, improving DNA precipitation yield, particularly in low-biomass samples.
Mix by inverting the tubes 50 times.
Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 16000 rcf, Room temperature, to pellet the DNA.

Carefully discard the supernatant by pouring into a waste container. Drain the tube by inverting on a paper towel. Take care that the pellet remains in the tube. A single, rapid inversion is essential. Allowing the liquid to settle back to the bottom can dislodge the DNA pellet, leading to loss of yield.
Mix by inverting the tubes 50 times.
Centrifuge for 00:01:00 at 16000 rcf, Room temperature, to pellet the DNA.
Washing the pellet once more: go to step #14 .
Carefully discard the supernatant by pouring into a waste container.

Transfer samples to a vacuum centrifuge. Dry the DNA pellets for 00:10:00 . If using a Vacufuge plus, use the D-AL setting. Overdrying the DNA pellet can significantly reduce its solubility. It is important to stop the drying process before the pellet becomes completely dry.
Vortex for 00:00:05 to break up the DNA pellet.
Choose 1: Incubate at 65 °C for 01:00:00 to dissolve the DNA. Incubate at Room temperature Overnight to dissolve the DNA.
Use immediately or store at: -80 °C for long-term storage -20 °C for use within a few weeks 4 °C for use within a few days
